qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multipart incremental-read quota bypass allows oversized requests to evade Wisp body and file limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable `multipart_body` and `multipart_headers` continuation branches as the advisory, where cross-chunk multipart parsing recurses without decrementing `max_body_size` or `max_files_size` quotas.

selected_finding.title: Multipart incremental-read quota bypass allows oversized requests to evade Wisp body and file limits

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's exact root cause in Wisp multipart parsing, and the copied PoC reproduced end-to-end acceptance of oversized multipart field and file data past configured quotas, which is the advisory-described uncontrolled resource-allocation behavior.
evidence: In `artifacts/validation_wisp_v221/src/wisp.gleam`, `multipart_body` recurses from `http.MoreRequiredForBody` at line 1172 and `multipart_headers` recurses from `http.MoreRequiredForHeaders` at line 1237 without decrementing quotas.

The fixed `v2.2.2` snapshot adds `decrement_quota(quota, size_read)` in the `MoreRequiredForBody` branch and `decrement_body_quota(quotas, size_read)` in the `MoreRequiredForHeaders` branch, matching the advisory's patch description.

The copied PoC run at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_bf387e08/.judge/poc_runs/multipart_chunk_quota_bypass/run.log` printed `BODY_LIMIT_BYPASSED value_len=200 limit=120` and `FILE_LIMIT_BYPASSED file_len=200 limit=120`, proving that multipart field and file content larger than the configured limits were accepted instead of rejected with `413 Content too large`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC accepted a 200-byte multipart field and a 200-byte multipart file despite 120-byte configured limits, printing `BODY_LIMIT_BYPASSED value_len=200 limit=120`, `FILE_LIMIT_BYPASSED file_len=200 limit=120`, and `VERIFICATION_PASSED multipart chunk quota bypass reproduced`.
